_source: BK-064 sub-deliverable 1. Per-amp master sweet-spot + relative-loudness corpus. Hand-curated from the Fractal-Audio-Blocks-Guide power-amp section, per-amp lineage records in fractal-midi shared/lineage, and community priors (Fractal Forum 'amp loudness' / 'master volume sweet spots' threads). See docs/research/loudness-data-methodology.md for full sourcing and precision discipline.
_referenceAmpRationale: Fender Twin Reverb (Double Verb in AM4 naming) is the canonical clean reference platform in guitar audio. 85W master-volume design with broad clean headroom. master=6.0 is the historical 'gig unity' setting cited across the Fractal Wiki and community threads.
_referenceDriveRationale: Ibanez TS-808 Tube Screamer is the de facto pedal-board reference. Blocks Guide and forum consensus put it at ~+6 dB perceived gain at drive.level=7 (default mid-position).
_precision: Plus or minus 3 dB. Suitable for agent leveling guidance, not mixing.

Double Verb Normal.notes: Reference amp for this table. Fender Twin Reverb 85W blackface. Clean-headroom platform; master=6 is gig-unity per forum consensus.

Double Verb Vibrato.notes: Twin Reverb vibrato channel. Same power section, same unity setting as Double Verb Normal.

Deluxe Verb Vibrato.notes: Fender Deluxe Reverb 22W. Smaller power section than Twin, breaks up earlier; gig-unity around master 5-6.

Princetone Reverb.notes: Fender Princeton Reverb 12W. Half the headroom of a Deluxe; runs hotter at lower master settings.

5F1 Tweed Champlifier.notes: 5F1 Tweed Champ has no master volume control (real-circuit model). Master is locked to 10.0 by design (per Blocks Guide page 9: non-master amps auto-set master to 10). 5W single-ended; quiet platform.

Supertweed.notes: Tweed Bassman-derived. Non-master amp, locked at 10; loud platform (4x10 design, ~50W).

59 Bassguy Normal.notes: Tweed Bassman 5F6-A reissue. Non-master amp (no master knob on the real amp). Gain knob acts as volume; very loud platform.

Deluxe Tweed.notes: Tweed Deluxe (5E3 / 5C3 era). Non-master amp; 15W. Famously breaks up early.

Class-A 30W.notes: Vox AC30 model. No master volume on the real amp; master is locked to 10. Class-A power section runs loud for its 30W rating.

Class-A 30W TB.notes: Vox AC30 Top Boost. No master volume; same loud-Class-A behavior as the base AC30 model.

Class-A 15W TB.notes: Vox AC15 TB. Half the power of the AC30; quieter platform. No master knob on the real amp.

1959SLP Normal.notes: Marshall 1959 Super Lead Plexi 100W reissue. Non-master amp; classic four-input Plexi. Very loud at unity; gain-staged via input drive instead of master.

1959SLP Treble.notes: 1959SLP treble channel. Same power section as Normal channel; treble-shifted voicing.

Plexi 100W High.notes: Marshall Plexi 100W (1959-derived). Non-master amp. Fractal's reference is a '69 SLP.

Plexi 50W Normal.notes: Marshall Plexi 50W (1987). Non-master amp; half the power of the 100W, still loud at unity.

Brit JM45.notes: Marshall JTM45. Non-master amp. ~30W KT66 platform; cleaner and quieter than Plexi but still loud per watt.

Brit Super.notes: Marshall Super Lead. Non-master amp; 100W EL34 design similar to Plexi.

Brit 800 2204 High.notes: Marshall JCM800 2204 (50W master volume amp). Pre-gain distortion design; master 4 is the classic 'club gig' unity. Cranking master past 6 gets harsh per Blocks Guide note on Negative Feedback.

Brit 800 2203 High.notes: Marshall JCM800 2203 (100W master volume). Hotter than the 2204 due to 100W power section.

Brit Silver.notes: Marshall Silver Jubilee. Master volume design; sits between 800 and modern voicings.

BRIT JVM OD1 ORANGE.notes: Marshall JVM OD1 orange channel. Modern master-volume Marshall; crunch territory at master 4.

Hipower Normal.notes: Hiwatt DR103 100W. Non-master amp; famously loud and clean for its 100W rating. Townshend / Pink Floyd platform.

Citrus A30 Clean.notes: Orange AD30HTC clean channel. Master volume design; mid-range clean headroom.

Citrus RV50.notes: Orange Rockerverb 50 MKII dirty channel. Master volume amp; classic high-gain platform sits around master 3-4.

Citrus Terrier.notes: Orange Tiny Terror. Master volume amp but small (15W); compressed and loud per watt. Often run with master high.

USA Pre Clean.notes: Mesa Studio Preamp clean channel. Preamp model (no real power amp); master scales the preamp output. Cleaner headroom than the lead channels.

USA MK IIC+.notes: Mesa Mark IIC+ lead channel. Famously hot preamp; master 2-3 is the classic Metallica / Petrucci unity. Gets shrill past 4.

USA MK IV Rhythm 1.notes: Mesa Mark IV rhythm 1 channel. Cleaner than IIC+ lead; master sits higher.

Recto2 Red Modern.notes: Mesa Dual Rectifier 2-channel red modern. Very hot preamp; master 2-3 is the touring norm. Cranking master compresses heavily.

Recto2 Orange Modern.notes: Mesa Dual Rectifier orange channel modern. Lower-gain rhythm voicing; master around 3.

PVH 6160+ Lead.notes: Peavey 6160+ / EVH 5150-iii lead channel. Hot preamp design; master 2-3 is gig unity. Extremely loud above 4.

FAS 6160.notes: Fractal-original 5150-class voicing. Same hot-preamp class as PVH 6160; same master sweet spot.

FRIEDMAN BE V2.notes: Friedman BE (Brown Eye) V2. Modded-Marshall lineage; master 3 is the classic gig setting per Friedman community.

FRIEDMAN HBE V2.notes: Friedman HBE (Hairy Brown Eye) V2. Hotter than BE; slightly louder at the same master setting.

Friedman Small Box.notes: Friedman Smallbox 50. Lower-gain master-volume amp; runs slightly cleaner than BE / HBE.

Dirty Shirley 1.notes: Friedman Dirty Shirley 40W. Master-volume amp; classic rock platform sits around master 4.

Solo 100 Lead.notes: Soldano SLO-100 lead channel. High-gain master-volume amp; SLO master is famously sensitive past 3.

DIZZY V4 BLUE 2.notes: Diezel VH4 channel 2 (blue / crunch). Master-volume amp; the VH4 is loud per setting and runs hot above 4.

Energyball.notes: Engl Powerball voicing. High-power high-gain modern amp; very loud at unity master.

Euro Red.notes: Bogner Ecstasy red channel (20th Anniversary). Master-volume amp; classic Bogner master sits 3-4.

Euro Uber.notes: Bogner Uberschall. Very hot high-gain amp; master 3 is gig norm, harsh above 5.

ODS-100 Ford.notes: Dumble Overdrive Special 100W (Robben Ford voicing). Master-volume amp with a wide clean-to-dirty range; master 4 is typical unity for OD.

T808 OD.notes: Reference drive for this table. Ibanez TS-808 Tube Screamer; mid-hump-boost circuit. Adds ~6 dB at drive.level=7 per Blocks Guide drive section and Fractal Forum consensus.

T808 Mod.notes: Modded TS-808 voicing. Similar boost response to stock 808.

Maxoff 808.notes: Maxon OD-808 (original-spec Tube Screamer). Same level response as T808.

Valve Screamer VS9.notes: Ibanez TS-9. Slightly brighter than the 808 but similar level response. Forum-cited identical perceived boost.

TS9DX Hot.notes: Ibanez TS9DX in hot mode. Higher gain and output than stock TS-9; adds ~8 dB at unity level.

TS9DX +.notes: Ibanez TS9DX in plus mode. Between stock TS-9 and hot mode for level.

Klone Chiron.notes: Klon Centaur / KTR clone. Transparent boost design; adds ~3-4 dB at drive.level=7. Pedal-builder consensus calls Klon perceived loudness change subtle.

Eternal Love.notes: Lovepedal Eternity. Klon-style transparent boost; similar low boost response to Klone.

Zen Master.notes: Hermida Zendrive. Smooth low-gain overdrive; ~4 dB perceived boost at typical settings.

Sunrise Splendor.notes: JHS Morning Glory derivative. Transparent OD; ~5 dB perceived boost.

Jam Ray.notes: Vemuram Jan Ray. Klon-adjacent transparent boost; low boost response.

Rat Distortion.notes: ProCo RAT. Heavy LM308-based distortion; significantly louder than the Tube Screamer family. Forum-cited ~9 dB.

Fat Rat.notes: Modded RAT with LED clipping. Similar output to stock RAT.

PI Fuzz.notes: Electro-Harmonix Big Muff Pi. Sustaining fuzz with high output; one of the loudest drive pedals on the board. Forum-cited 10+ dB.

PI FUZZ - RAM'S HEAD.notes: 70s Big Muff Pi Ram's Head reissue. Similar high-output behavior to modern Big Muff.

PI FUZZ - TRIANGLE.notes: Triangle-era Big Muff Pi. High-output fuzz; same class as the other Big Muff variants.

DS1 Distortion.notes: Boss DS-1. Hotter and more distorted than TS-808; +8 dB perceived at typical settings.

Super OD.notes: Boss SD-1 Super Overdrive. Tube-Screamer-adjacent voicing with asymmetric clipping; similar +6 dB perceived boost.

OD-One Overdrive.notes: Boss OD-1. The original Boss overdrive; slightly lower output than SD-1 / TS-808.

Blues OD.notes: Marshall Bluesbreaker Mk1. Low-gain transparent overdrive; ~5 dB perceived boost.

BB Pre.notes: Xotic BB Preamp. Tube Screamer family with a JFET-buffered output; ~6 dB at unity.

COMPULSION DISTORTION HP.notes: Fulltone OCD high-peak (HP) mode. Higher output than tube screamer family; ~7 dB perceived.

COMPULSION DISTORTION LP.notes: Fulltone OCD low-peak (LP) mode. Slightly lower output than HP mode.

FAS Boost.notes: Fractal-original clean boost. Designed for unity-or-louder front-of-amp use; ~+6 dB at level=7.

Micro Boost.notes: MXR Micro Amp clone. Transparent clean boost; ~6 dB perceived.

Face Fuzz.notes: Dallas Arbiter Fuzz Face. Germanium fuzz; output depends heavily on input impedance but +7 dB is a fair average at typical settings.

Bender Fuzz.notes: Tone Bender / Vox Tone Bender. Similar output class to Fuzz Face.

M-Zone Distortion.notes: Boss Metal Zone. Heavy mid-scooped distortion; high output; ~8 dB perceived.

Angry Chuck.notes: Friedman Angry Charlie. JCM800-in-a-box style drive; ~6 dB perceived boost.

OD 250.notes: DOD 250 / MXR Distortion+. Hot op-amp distortion; ~7 dB perceived.

Treble Boost.notes: Dallas Rangemaster / silicon treble booster. Treble-shifted boost; ~5 dB perceived but voiced for treble bite.

Griddle Cake.notes: Crowther Hot Cake. Low-gain transparent OD; ~5 dB perceived.

MCMLXXXI DRV.notes: 1981 Inventions DRV. Modern transparent low-gain OD; ~5 dB perceived.
